name: Ical

unions.name: JournalStatus
unions.comment:
Journal status per
[RFC 5545 Section 3.8.1.11](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5545#section-3.8.1.11).

  - `Draft` — the journal entry is a draft.
  - `Final` — the journal entry is final.
  - `CancelledJournal` — the journal entry has been cancelled.
    (Named `CancelledJournal` to avoid a clash with [`Cancelled`](#Status).)

unions.name: Status
unions.comment:
The scheduling status of an event.

  - `Tentative` — the event is not yet confirmed.
  - `Confirmed` — the event is definite.
  - `Cancelled` — the event has been cancelled.

Per [RFC 5545 Section 3.8.1.11](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5545#section-3.8.1.11).

unions.name: Transparency
unions.comment:
Whether the event blocks time on a calendar for free/busy lookups.

  - `Opaque` — the event blocks time (default in iCal).
  - `Transparent` — the event does not block time (e.g. reminders, FYI events).

Per [RFC 5545 Section 3.8.2.7](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5545#section-3.8.2.7).

name: Ical.Generator
comment:
Generate iCal ([RFC 5545](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5545)) calendar feeds
from typed Elm values.

    import Date
    import Ical
    import Ical.Generator as Generator
    import Ical.Recurrence as Recurrence
    import Time

    teamCalendar : Time.Posix -> String
    teamCalendar now =
        let
            weeklySync : Generator.Event
            weeklySync =
                Generator.event
                    { id = "weekly-sync"
                    , stamp = now
                    , time =
                        Generator.timedEvent
                            { start = mar18at10am
                            , end = mar18at11am
                            }
                    , summary = "Weekly Team Sync"
                    }
                    |> Generator.withRecurrenceRule
                        (Generator.rule (Recurrence.Weekly { every = 1, weekStart = Time.Mon }))

            offsite : Generator.Event
            offsite =
                Generator.event
                    { id = "offsite-q2"
                    , stamp = now
                    , time =
                        Generator.allDayRange
                            { start = Date.fromCalendarDate 2021 Time.Jun 14
                            , end = Date.fromCalendarDate 2021 Time.Jun 16
                            }
                    , summary = "Q2 Team Offsite"
                    }
                    |> Generator.withLocation "Moscone Center, 747 Howard St, San Francisco, CA 94103"
        in
        Generator.generate
            (Generator.config
                { id = "//mycompany//team//EN"
                , domain = "mycompany.com"
                }
                |> Generator.withName "Engineering Team"
            )
            { events = [ weeklySync, offsite ]
            , journals = []
            }

    mar18at10am : Time.Posix
    mar18at10am =
        Time.millisToPosix 1616065200000

    mar18at11am : Time.Posix
    mar18at11am =
        Time.millisToPosix 1616068800000

All generation types are opaque with builder functions. Invalid inputs like
reversed start/end times, negative intervals, or incompatible recurrence
parts are silently normalized.


## Generating output

@docs generate, generateEvent


## Calendar configuration

@docs Config, config, withName, withCalendarDescription, withUrl


## Events

@docs Event, event, EventTime, allDay, allDayRange, timedEvent, floatingTime, FloatingDateTime, Organizer, Attendee
@docs withDescription, withLocation, withOrganizer, withHtmlDescription
@docs withStatus, withTransparency
@docs withCreated, withLastModified
@docs withRecurrenceRule, withAttendee
@docs withAlarm, Alarm, displayAlarm, audioAlarm, AlarmTrigger


## Journals

@docs Journal, journal, JournalTime
@docs withJournalDescription, withJournalDate, withJournalTime, withJournalFloatingTime
@docs withJournalStatus
@docs withJournalCreated, withJournalLastModified, withJournalOrganizer


## Recurrence rules

@docs Rule, rule, withCount, withUntilDate, withUntilDateTime
@docs withByDay, withByMonthDay, withByMonth, withBySetPos
@docs withByHour, withByMinute, withBySecond, withByYearDay, withByWeekNo

unions.name: Alarm
unions.comment:
An opaque alarm (VALARM). Create one with [`displayAlarm`](#displayAlarm)
or [`audioAlarm`](#audioAlarm), then attach to an event with
[`withAlarm`](#withAlarm).

The RFC also defines EMAIL alarms, but in practice they are rarely supported
by modern calendar clients (Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, Outlook all use
push notifications instead). Only DISPLAY and AUDIO are provided here.

unions.name: AlarmTrigger
unions.comment:
When an alarm fires, as a signed offset in seconds. Negative values mean
before, positive values mean after.

    -- 15 minutes before the event starts
    SecondsFromStart (-15 * 60)

    -- 1 day before the event ends
    SecondsFromEnd (-24 * 60 * 60)

    -- at the moment the event starts
    SecondsFromStart 0

unions.name: Config
unions.comment:
An opaque type representing calendar configuration. Create one with
[`config`](#config) and customize with [`withName`](#withName),
[`withCalendarDescription`](#withCalendarDescription), and [`withUrl`](#withUrl).

unions.name: Event
unions.comment:
An opaque type representing a calendar event. Create one with [`event`](#event)
and customize it with the `with*` functions.

unions.name: EventTime
unions.comment:
Represents the time span of an event. Create values with [`allDay`](#allDay),
[`allDayRange`](#allDayRange), [`timedEvent`](#timedEvent), or
[`floatingTime`](#floatingTime).

unions.name: Journal
unions.comment:
An opaque type representing a journal entry. Create one with
[`journal`](#journal) and customize with the `withJournal*` functions.

unions.name: JournalTime
unions.comment:
The time of a journal entry. Journals have no time span, just an optional
date or datetime stamp.

unions.name: Rule
unions.comment:
An opaque recurrence rule. Create one with [`rule`](#rule) and customize
with the `with*` builder functions.

    Generator.rule (Recurrence.Weekly { every = 1, weekStart = Time.Mon })
        |> Generator.withByDay [ Recurrence.every Time.Mon ]
        |> Generator.withCount 10

aliases.name: Attendee
aliases.comment: A person with a name and email address, used for the ATTENDEE property.

aliases.name: FloatingDateTime
aliases.comment:
A local date-time with no timezone. Used for floating-time events that
represent the same wall-clock time regardless of the viewer's timezone.

    { date = Date.fromCalendarDate 2021 Time.Mar 18
    , hour = 14
    , minute = 0
    , second = 0
    }

Out-of-range values are clamped: hour to 0–23, minute to 0–59, second to 0–59.
aliases.type: { date : Date.Date, hour : Basics.Int, minute : Basics.Int, second : Basics.Int }

aliases.name: Organizer
aliases.comment: A person with a name and email address, used for the ORGANIZER property.

values.name: allDay
values.comment:
Create a single-day all-day event.

    Generator.allDay (Date.fromCalendarDate 2021 Time.Mar 18)

The library automatically adds one day to produce the exclusive DTEND
required by iCal.

values.name: allDayRange
values.comment:
Create a multi-day all-day event time span. The `end` date is **inclusive**.
The library automatically adds one day to produce the exclusive DTEND
required by iCal.

    Generator.allDayRange
        { start = Date.fromCalendarDate 2021 Time.Jun 14
        , end = Date.fromCalendarDate 2021 Time.Jun 16
        }

If `end` is before `start`, the dates are swapped automatically.

values.name: audioAlarm
values.comment:
Create an audio alarm that plays a sound.

    Generator.audioAlarm
        { trigger = Generator.SecondsFromStart (-15 * 60)
        }

values.name: config
values.comment:
Create a calendar configuration with the required fields.

  - `id` — identifies your product in the PRODID property
    (e.g. `"//mycompany//myapp//EN"`).
  - `domain` — your domain, used to generate globally unique event UIDs
    (e.g. `"example.com"` produces UIDs like `"event-id@example.com"`).

```
Generator.config { id = "//mycompany//myapp//EN", domain = "example.com" }
```

values.name: displayAlarm
values.comment:
Create a display alarm that shows a text notification.

    Generator.displayAlarm
        { description = "Meeting in 15 minutes"
        , trigger = Generator.SecondsFromStart (-15 * 60)
        }

values.name: event
values.comment:
Create an event with the required fields.

    Generator.event
        { id = "unique-id-123"
        , stamp = timestamp
        , time = Generator.timedEvent { start = startTime, end = endTime }
        , summary = "Team Meeting"
        }
values.type: { id : String.String, stamp : Time.Posix, time : Ical.Generator.EventTime, summary : String.String } -> Ical.Generator.Event

values.name: floatingTime
generate
values.comment:
Generate a complete iCal calendar string with the given config, events,
and journals.

    Generator.generate
        (Generator.config { id = "//myapp//EN", domain = "example.com" })
        { events = [ weeklySync ]
        , journals = [ dailyNotes ]
        }
values.type: Ical.Generator.Config -> { events : List.List Ical.Generator.Event, journals : List.List Ical.Generator.Journal } -> String.String

values.name: generateEvent
values.comment:
Generate the iCal string for a single VEVENT component, without the
surrounding VCALENDAR wrapper. Useful for embedding in a larger calendar
you are building up. For a complete iCal document, use [`generate`](#generate).

values.name: journal
values.comment:
Create a journal entry with the required fields.

    Generator.journal
        { id = "journal-1"
        , stamp = timestamp
        , summary = "Daily standup notes"
        }
values.type: { id : String.String, stamp : Time.Posix, summary : String.String } -> Ical.Generator.Journal

values.name: rule
values.comment:
Create a recurrence rule with the given frequency. Negative `every` values
are clamped to 1.

    Generator.rule (Recurrence.Weekly { every = 1, weekStart = Time.Mon })
        |> Generator.withByDay [ Recurrence.every Time.Mon ]
        |> Generator.withCount 10

values.name: timedEvent
values.comment:
Create a timed event time span with UTC start and end times.

If `end` is before `start`, the times are swapped automatically.

values.name: withAlarm
withAttendee
withByDay
values.comment:
Set which days of the week the rule applies to.

Ordinal day specs are kept only for monthly rules and yearly rules without
`BYWEEKNO`. In other frequencies they are dropped.

values.name: withByHour
values.comment:
Set which hours the rule applies to. Valid values are 0–23. Invalid values are dropped.
This part is dropped for all-day events when the rule is attached.

values.name: withByMinute
values.comment:
Set which minutes the rule applies to. Valid values are 0–59. Invalid values are dropped.
This part is dropped for all-day events when the rule is attached.

values.name: withByMonth
values.comment:
Set which months the rule applies to.

    Generator.rule (Recurrence.Yearly { every = 1 })
        |> Generator.withByMonth [ Time.Jan, Time.Apr, Time.Jul, Time.Oct ]

values.name: withByMonthDay
values.comment:
Set which days of the month the rule applies to. Valid values are
-31 to -1 and 1 to 31. Negative values count from the end of the month
(e.g. -1 is the last day). Invalid values are dropped. This part is also
dropped for weekly rules.

values.name: withBySecond
values.comment:
Set which seconds the rule applies to. Valid values are 0–60 (60 for leap second).
Invalid values are dropped. This part is dropped for all-day events when the
rule is attached.

values.name: withBySetPos
values.comment:
Filter to specific positions within each recurrence period. For example,
with `BYDAY=MO,TU,WE,TH,FR`, a `bySetPos` of `[ -1 ]` means "the last weekday."
Invalid values are dropped. If no other `BY*` filters remain, `BYSETPOS` is
dropped too.

values.name: withByWeekNo
values.comment:
Set which ISO week numbers the rule applies to. Valid values are
1 to 53 and -53 to -1. Negative values count from the end of the year.
Invalid values are dropped. This part is dropped unless the rule frequency is
yearly.

values.name: withByYearDay
values.comment:
Set which days of the year the rule applies to. Valid values are
1 to 366 and -366 to -1. Negative values count from the end of the year.
Invalid values are dropped. This part is dropped for daily, weekly, and
monthly rules.

values.name: withCalendarDescription
values.comment: Set the calendar description.

values.name: withCount
values.comment: Set a count limit. Values less than 1 are clamped to 1.

values.name: withCreated
values.comment: Set the creation timestamp.

values.name: withDescription
values.comment: Set a plain-text description for the event.

values.name: withHtmlDescription
values.comment:
Set an HTML-formatted description. Calendar apps that support rich text
will display this instead of the plain-text description.

values.name: withJournalCreated
values.comment: Set the journal creation timestamp.

values.name: withJournalDate
values.comment: Set the journal date (all-day).

values.name: withJournalDescription
values.comment: Set a plain-text description for the journal entry.

values.name: withJournalFloatingTime
values.comment: Set the journal time as a floating local datetime.

values.name: withJournalLastModified
values.comment: Set the journal last-modified timestamp.

values.name: withJournalOrganizer
values.comment: Set the journal organizer.

values.name: withJournalStatus
values.comment: Set the journal status. Uses the shared [`JournalStatus`](Ical#JournalStatus) type.

values.name: withJournalTime
values.comment: Set the journal time (UTC).

values.name: withLastModified
values.comment: Set the last-modified timestamp.

values.name: withLocation
values.comment:
Set the event location (LOCATION property). Use a full, resolved address
so calendar apps can map it directly:

    Generator.event
        { id = "offsite-q2"
        , stamp = now
        , time = Generator.allDayRange { start = jun14, end = jun16 }
        , summary = "Q2 Team Offsite"
        }
        |> Generator.withLocation "Moscone Center, 747 Howard St, San Francisco, CA 94103"

values.name: withName
values.comment: Set the calendar display name.

values.name: withOrganizer
values.comment: Set the event organizer.

values.name: withRecurrenceRule
values.comment:
Add a recurrence rule to the event.

    Generator.event { ... }
        |> Generator.withRecurrenceRule
            (Generator.rule (Recurrence.Weekly { every = 1, weekStart = Time.Mon })
                |> Generator.withCount 10
            )

Incompatible rule parts are dropped to match the event time shape. For
example, all-day events do not keep `BYHOUR`, `BYMINUTE`, or `BYSECOND`.

values.name: withStatus
values.comment: Set the event status. Uses the shared [`Status`](Ical#Status) type.

values.name: withTransparency
values.comment: Set the event transparency. Uses the shared [`Transparency`](Ical#Transparency) type.

values.name: withUntilDate
values.comment: Set the recurrence end to a specific date.

values.name: withUntilDateTime
values.comment: Set the recurrence end to a specific date-time.

values.name: withUrl
values.comment: Set the calendar URL.

name: Ical.Parser
comment:
Parse iCal ([RFC 5545](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5545)) calendar strings
into typed Elm data.

    import Date
    import Ical.Parser as Parser

    holidayFeedToString : String -> String
    holidayFeedToString icsString =
        case Parser.parse icsString of
            Ok cal ->
                cal.events
                    |> List.filterMap toHoliday
                    |> List.sortBy
                        (.date >> Date.toRataDie)
                    |> List.map
                        (\h ->
                            Date.format "EEE, MMM d" h.date
                                ++ " - "
                                ++ h.name
                        )
                    |> String.join "\n"

            Err err ->
                "Parse error: " ++ err

    toHoliday :
        Parser.Event
        -> Maybe { name : String, date : Date.Date }
    toHoliday event =
        case ( event.summary, event.time ) of
            ( Just name, Parser.AllDay { start } ) ->
                Just { name = name, date = start }

            _ ->
                Nothing

    -- holidayFeedToString usHolidaysFeed
    --
    --   Thu, Jan 1 - New Year's Day
    --   Mon, Jan 19 - MLK Day
    --   ...

See the [`examples/` folder](https://github.com/dillonkearns/elm-ical/tree/main/examples)
for a full runnable script that fetches and parses a live Google Calendar feed.

Parsing is strict: malformed content lines, invalid dates and times, bad
`RRULE` values, and a `TZID` without a matching `VTIMEZONE` all return `Err`.
Parsed types are transparent record aliases so you can read fields directly.


## Parsing

@docs parse
@docs Calendar, Event


## Event times

@docs EventTime, OccurrenceReference, ResolvedTime, LocalDateTime, TimeZoneContext


## Enums and metadata

@docs Organizer, RawProperty
@docs Attendee, AttendeeRole, ParticipationStatus


## Alarms

@docs Alarm, AlarmAction, AlarmTrigger, AlarmTriggerRelative


## Journals

@docs Journal, JournalTime


## Recurrence expansion

@docs Occurrence, expand, expandNext

unions.name: AlarmAction
unions.comment: The type of alarm.

unions.name: AlarmTrigger
unions.comment:
When the alarm fires.

  - `TriggerDuration` — a relative offset from the event start or end.
  - `TriggerAbsolute` — a fixed UTC time (used with `VALUE=DATE-TIME`).

unions.name: AlarmTriggerRelative
unions.comment:
A relative alarm trigger as a signed offset in seconds.
Negative values mean before, positive values mean after.

    -- 15 minutes before start
    SecondsFromStart (-15 * 60)

    -- at the moment the event ends
    SecondsFromEnd 0

unions.name: AttendeeRole
unions.comment:
Attendee role per
[RFC 5545 Section 3.2.16](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5545#section-3.2.16).

unions.name: EventTime
unions.comment:
The time span of an event. The variant determines what type of values
`start` and `end` carry, enforcing the
[RFC 5545](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5545#section-3.8.2.2)
requirement that DTSTART and DTEND share the same value type.

The `end` is always populated. When neither DTEND nor DURATION is present in
the source data, the parser applies the RFC 5545 default: DATE events end on
the following day, and DATE-TIME events end at the same instant they start.

  - `AllDay`: `VALUE=DATE`; the event spans whole days.
  - `WithTime`: a resolved instant in time (UTC or TZID resolved via VTIMEZONE).
  - `FloatingTime`: a local date-time with no timezone; interpreted in the
    viewer's local time.

unions.name: JournalTime
unions.comment:
The time of a journal entry. Since journals have no time span (no DTEND
or DURATION), this is a single point — either a date or a datetime.

unions.name: OccurrenceReference
unions.comment:
A typed reference to a specific recurrence instance as used by `EXDATE`,
`RDATE`, and `RECURRENCE-ID`.

The constructor matches the value type from the iCalendar data:

  - `OnDate` for `VALUE=DATE`
  - `AtTime` for resolved `DATE-TIME` values (UTC or TZID-backed)
  - `AtFloatingTime` for floating local `DATE-TIME` values

unions.name: ParticipationStatus
unions.comment:
Attendee participation status per
[RFC 5545 Section 3.2.12](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5545#section-3.2.12).

unions.name: TimeZoneContext
unions.comment:
Opaque timezone data retained for parsed TZID date-times so recurrence
expansion can resolve future local wall-clock occurrences correctly.

Most code will only need [`ResolvedTime.posix`](#ResolvedTime) and
[`ResolvedTime.timeZoneName`](#ResolvedTime).

aliases.name: Alarm
aliases.comment:
A parsed alarm (VALARM) attached to an event.

Each alarm has an `action` (`Display` or `Audio`), a `trigger` (when the alarm
fires), and an optional `description`.

    event.alarms
        |> List.map .trigger
        -- [ TriggerDuration (SecondsFromStart (-900)), ... ]
aliases.type: { action : Ical.Parser.AlarmAction, trigger : Ical.Parser.AlarmTrigger, description : Maybe.Maybe String.String }

aliases.name: Attendee
aliases.type: { email : String.String, name : Maybe.Maybe String.String, role : Ical.Parser.AttendeeRole, participationStatus : Ical.Parser.ParticipationStatus, rsvp : Basics.Bool }

aliases.name: Calendar
aliases.comment:
A parsed iCal calendar. Access fields directly:

    case Parser.parse icsString of
        Ok cal ->
            List.length cal.events

        Err err ->
            0
aliases.type: { generatorProductId : String.String, specVersion : String.String, events : List.List Ical.Parser.Event, journals : List.List Ical.Parser.Journal, extraProperties : List.List Ical.Parser.RawProperty }

aliases.name: Event
aliases.comment:
A parsed iCal event.

The `uid`, `stamp`, and `time` fields are required by
[RFC 5545 Section 3.6.1](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5545#section-3.6.1).
Parsing will fail if DTSTART is missing from a VEVENT.

The `end` inside `time` is always populated — from DTEND, DURATION (which are
[mutually exclusive](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5545#section-3.6.1)
per the spec), or the RFC default when neither is present:

  - DATE events default to ending on the following date
  - DATE-TIME events default to ending at the same instant they start
aliases.type: { uid : String.String, stamp : Time.Posix, time : Ical.Parser.EventTime, created : Maybe.Maybe Time.Posix, lastModified : Maybe.Maybe Time.Posix, summary : Maybe.Maybe String.String, description : Maybe.Maybe String.String, location : Maybe.Maybe String.String, organizer : Maybe.Maybe Ical.Parser.Organizer, status : Maybe.Maybe Ical.Status, transparency : Maybe.Maybe Ical.Transparency, recurrenceRules : List.List Ical.Recurrence.RecurrenceRule, exclusions : List.List Ical.Parser.OccurrenceReference, recurrenceDates : List.List Ical.Parser.OccurrenceReference, recurrenceId : Maybe.Maybe Ical.Parser.OccurrenceReference, attendees : List.List Ical.Parser.Attendee, alarms : List.List Ical.Parser.Alarm, extraProperties : List.List Ical.Parser.RawProperty }

aliases.name: Journal
aliases.comment:
A parsed iCal journal entry
([RFC 5545 Section 3.6.3](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5545#section-3.6.3)).
Unlike events, journals have no time span — just an optional date or datetime
stamp and text content. They represent dated notes or log entries.
aliases.type: { uid : String.String, stamp : Time.Posix, time : Maybe.Maybe Ical.Parser.JournalTime, summary : Maybe.Maybe String.String, description : Maybe.Maybe String.String, status : Maybe.Maybe Ical.JournalStatus, created : Maybe.Maybe Time.Posix, lastModified : Maybe.Maybe Time.Posix, organizer : Maybe.Maybe Ical.Parser.Organizer, extraProperties : List.List Ical.Parser.RawProperty }

aliases.name: LocalDateTime
aliases.comment:
A date-time without timezone information. Represents a "floating" local
date-time that is interpreted in the viewer's local time.
aliases.type: { year : Basics.Int, month : Time.Month, day : Basics.Int, hour : Basics.Int, minute : Basics.Int, second : Basics.Int }

aliases.name: Occurrence
aliases.comment:
A single occurrence of an event. For non-recurring events, there is one
occurrence whose `time` matches `event.time`. For recurring events, each
occurrence has its own `time` shifted to the recurrence date while preserving
the original event's duration.

aliases.name: RawProperty
aliases.comment:
An uninterpreted property: name, parameters, and raw value string.
Unknown properties and extension properties (`X-*`) are preserved here.
aliases.type: { name : String.String, parameters : List.List ( String.String, String.String ), value : String.String }

aliases.name: ResolvedTime
aliases.comment:
A resolved date-time instant.

The `timeZoneName` is `Nothing` for UTC datetimes, or a
[IANA Time Zone Database](https://www.iana.org/time-zones) name
(e.g. `Just "America/New_York"`) for TZID-resolved datetimes.

For TZID values, `localDateTime` retains the original wall-clock datetime and
`timeZoneContext` keeps the parsed timezone definition so recurrence expansion
can stay in local calendar dates. Most consumers can ignore those fields and
just use `posix`.
aliases.type: { posix : Time.Posix, timeZoneName : Maybe.Maybe String.String, localDateTime : Maybe.Maybe Ical.Parser.LocalDateTime, timeZoneContext : Maybe.Maybe Ical.Parser.TimeZoneContext }

values.name: expand
values.comment:
Expand events into concrete occurrences within a date range, merging
RECURRENCE-ID overrides automatically. Override events (those with a
`recurrenceId`) replace the matching occurrence from the master event that
shares their `uid`.

    case Parser.parse icsString of
        Ok cal ->
            Parser.expand { start = jan1, end = dec31 } cal.events

        Err err ->
            []

For a single event, wrap it in a list:

    Parser.expand { start = jan1, end = dec31 } [ event ]
values.type: { start : Date.Date, end : Date.Date } -> List.List Ical.Parser.Event -> List.List Ical.Parser.Occurrence

values.name: expandNext
values.comment:
Get the next N occurrences starting from a given date, merging
RECURRENCE-ID overrides automatically.

    case Parser.parse icsString of
        Ok cal ->
            Parser.expandNext 10 today cal.events

        Err err ->
            []

For a single event, wrap it in a list:

    Parser.expandNext 5 today [ event ]
values.type: Basics.Int -> Date.Date -> List.List Ical.Parser.Event -> List.List Ical.Parser.Occurrence

values.name: parse

name: Ical.Recurrence

unions.name: DaySpec
unions.comment:
A day-of-week specifier for recurrence rules.

Create one with [`every`](#every), [`nth`](#nth), or the convenience helpers
like [`second`](#second) and [`last`](#last).

    -- every Monday
    every Time.Mon

    -- the 2nd Sunday of the month
    second Time.Sun

    -- the last Friday
    last Time.Fri

Positive ordinals count from the start of the period. Negative ordinals count
from the end. `nth` accepts `-53..-1` and `1..53`, matching the RFC grammar
for numeric `BYDAY` values.

unions.name: Frequency
unions.comment:
How often the event recurs. Each variant carries its own configuration:

  - `every` — the interval between occurrences (e.g. `every = 2` with `Weekly`
    means every other week). Must be at least 1.
  - `weekStart` — which day begins the week (only meaningful for `Weekly`).

unions.name: RecurrenceEnd
unions.comment:
When the recurrence stops. `Count` and `Until*` are mutually exclusive per
[RFC 5545 Section 3.3.10](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5545#section-3.3.10).

aliases.name: RecurrenceRule
aliases.comment:
A parsed recurrence rule (RRULE). This is the transparent record returned
by [`Ical.Parser`](Ical-Parser) for reading parsed rule data. For constructing
rules for generation, use [`Ical.Generator.Rule`](Ical-Generator#Rule) and its builder functions.

    { frequency = Weekly { every = 1, weekStart = Time.Mon }
    , end = Forever
    , byDay = [ every Time.Mon ]
    , byMonthDay = []
    , byMonth = []
    , bySetPos = []
    , byHour = []
    , byMinute = []
    , bySecond = []
    , byYearDay = []
    , byWeekNo = []
    }

  - `byDay` — which days of the week (with optional ordinals for monthly/yearly).
  - `byMonthDay` — which days of the month (1–31, or -31 to -1 from month end).
  - `byMonth` — which months.
  - `bySetPos` — pick the Nth result from the set produced by the other `by*`
    parts within each frequency period. Positive counts from the start, negative
    from the end. For example, `BYDAY=MO,TU,WE,TH,FR` with `bySetPos = [ -1 ]`
    means "the last weekday" of each period.
  - `byHour` — which hours (0–23).
  - `byMinute` — which minutes (0–59).
  - `bySecond` — which seconds (0–60, 60 for leap second).
  - `byYearDay` — which days of the year (1–366, or -366 to -1 from year end).
  - `byWeekNo` — which ISO week numbers (1–53, or -53 to -1 from year end).
aliases.type: { frequency : Ical.Recurrence.Frequency, end : Ical.Recurrence.RecurrenceEnd, byDay : List.List Ical.Recurrence.DaySpec, byMonthDay : List.List Basics.Int, byMonth : List.List Time.Month, bySetPos : List.List Basics.Int, byHour : List.List Basics.Int, byMinute : List.List Basics.Int, bySecond : List.List Basics.Int, byYearDay : List.List Basics.Int, byWeekNo : List.List Basics.Int }

values.name: every
values.comment: Match every instance of the given weekday.

values.name: fifth
values.comment: The 5th instance of a weekday.

values.name: fifthToLast
values.comment: The 5th-to-last instance of a weekday.

values.name: first
values.comment: The 1st instance of a weekday.

values.name: fourth
values.comment: The 4th instance of a weekday.

values.name: fourthToLast
values.comment: The 4th-to-last instance of a weekday.

values.name: last
values.comment: The last instance of a weekday.

values.name: nth
values.comment:
Match the `n`th instance of a weekday.

Positive values count from the start, negative values count from the end, and
`0` is invalid.

    nth 20 Time.Mon

    nth -1 Time.Fri

values.name: ordinal
values.comment: Extract the ordinal from a `DaySpec`, if present.

values.name: second
values.comment: The 2nd instance of a weekday.

values.name: secondToLast
values.comment: The 2nd-to-last instance of a weekday.

values.name: third
values.comment: The 3rd instance of a weekday.

values.name: thirdToLast
values.comment: The 3rd-to-last instance of a weekday.

values.name: weekday
values.comment: Extract the weekday from a `DaySpec`.
